name: Classifying daily activities needs posture, reconstructing them needs motion

description: arXiv:2607.13216v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Humans recognize movements effortlessly, even from noisy and complex visual input. But what information in the stimulus allows humans to rapidly classify movements? No framework has systematically compared different strategies of movement analysis to address this question. Here, we used videos of 16 daily activities from the MoVi dataset and com...
author: Arefeh Farahmandi, Gunnar Blohm
